(...) : null}` would fail despite being functionally equivale

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The two fail-to-pass tests (test_css_description_first_child_spacing and test_pure_content_conditional_title_rendering) are purely text-matching: they open files and regex-search for specific string patterns. They do not import, call, render, or execute any code to verify the actual behavior. The pass-to-pass tests do run subprocesses (jest, eslint, biome), but the core fail-to-pass tests are entirely `assert pattern in open(path).read()` style.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests only check for text patterns in source files. Simply adding the exact string `'&:first-child': { marginInlineEnd: token.marginSM` to the style file and `{ title && (` to PurePanel.tsx (even as dead code or comments) would pass these tests without actually fixing the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: (1) conditional title rendering, (2) description first-child spacing, (3) existing tests still pass. All three are covered by the test suite (tests 1-2 for the fixes, tests 3-7 for regression).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names and docstrings that clearly indicate FAIL-TO-PASS vs PASS-TO-PASS and explain what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves fixing a UI component bug, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in the task files are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names both files to edit (PurePanel.tsx and style/index.ts) and describes the exact nature of the fix needed in each (conditional rendering pattern for title, first-child CSS rule for description). While naming the component directory is reasonable, specifying both exact files AND the exact approach to fix them is excessive leakage.
